items.headline: background sessions stabilized in Claude Code 2.1.176
items.body: Claude Code 2.1.176 ships fixes for background sessions and Remote Control alongside enhanced auto-mode fallback behavior. the background session fixes matter for this portfolio's agent system — scout and reviewer both run headless via claude -p, making session stability load-bearing. the auto-mode fallback change is worth watching: if model-tier selection shifts between runs, headless agents could behave differently than interactive sessions in ways that are hard to reproduce.

items.source_title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.headline: export controls can kill frontier model access
items.body: the US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 globally; Anthropic is pushing back but compliance is immediate. this is a new failure mode for any project that assumes a specific frontier model tier will be available. ADO MCP Server and LaborQuest both call Claude directly — explicit model pinning with a tested fallback is now the conservative choice, not an edge-case precaution.
